leadText: Geomancy (Japanese: ジオコントロール Geo Control) is a non-damaging Fairy-type move introduced in Generation VI. It is the signature move of Xerneas.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation VIII 1.1.1 Pokémon Sword and Shield 1.1.2 Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.2 Generation IX 1.2.1 Pokémon Scarlet and Violet 1.2.2 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] The user charges the move on the first turn. On the second turn, the user's Special Attack , Special Defense , and Speed increase by two stages. The held item Power Herb can allow its holder to use Geomancy in one turn, then the Power Herb is consumed in the process. If the user falls for Taunt while charging, the move will fail on the next turn. If powered up by a Fairium Z into Z-Geomancy, the user's Attack , Defense , Special Attack , Special Defense , and Speed are raised by one stage each (before Geomancy's charging turn). If the user is under the effect of Taunt , Z-Geomancy's initial stat-boosting effect as well as its charging turn will execute normally, but the move will fail after the charging turn. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Sword and Shield [ edit | edit source ] In Versions 1.0 to 1.2.1 of Pokémon Sword and Shield , Geomancy could not be selected in a battle. Starting with Version 1.3.0, it is usable and functions the same as it did in previous generations. Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl [ edit | edit source ] Geomancy cannot be selected in a battle. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Scarlet and Violet [ edit | edit source ] Geomancy cannot be selected in a battle. Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Status Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power — Cooldown 30s Move wind-up time 80 frames 70 frames Effect duration 20s 23s Additional effect — — — None

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh LA The user absorbs energy and sharply raises its Sp. Atk, Sp. Def, and Speed stats on the next turn. BD SP This move can't be used. It's recommended that this move is forgotten. Once forgotten, this move can't be remembered. S V The user absorbs energy on the first turn, then sharply boosts its Sp. Atk, Sp. Def, and Speed stats on the next turn. ZA The user absorbs energy to boost its Sp. Atk and Sp. Def stats and increase its movement speed for a while. This move takes some time to carry out.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S MD It sharply boosts your teammates' Sp. Atk, Sp. Def, and Travel Speed the next turn. * You'll get the Geomancy status condition, which lets you charge power, and then sharply boosts the Sp. Atk, Sp. Def, and Travel Speed of your party in the same room. The stats return to normal when you go to the next floor or step on a Wonder Tile. *

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Geomancy is the only status move that has a charging turn . It is also the only status move that is the signature move of a game mascot . Excluding moves that were only signature moves in certain generations, it is the only signature move to be a Fast Attack in Pokémon GO . When Xerneas uses Geomancy in Super Smash Bros. Ultimate , the sound effect of a Super Mushroom / Fire Flower from Super Mario Bros. can be heard.
